metadata.title: Cambridge Movers Gold Mock 2
metadata.description: Manually authored A1 Movers gold mock 2 — full 51-item form with Weather, Jobs, and Technology themes aligned to the M2.0 blueprint.
metadata.authoringNotes: M4.1 Gold Mock — manually authored and academically reviewed.

audio.transcript:
Examiner: Look at the pictures. Listen and draw a line from each name to the correct person.

This is Emma. She's holding a red umbrella because it's raining.
Sam is working on the building site. He's wearing a yellow hard hat.
Lily always uses a laptop in computer class.
Ben is pointing at the weather map on the TV screen.
Zoe's got small tools and she's fixing a computer.
Jake is standing between two computer screens in the office.
audio.caption: Link names to descriptions

audio.transcript:
Examiner: Listen and write the missing word or number.

The temperature today is twenty degrees.
My dad is an engineer and he builds bridges.
Look outside. The weather is cloudy and grey.
There are eight computers in the technology shop.
She is downloading a new game on her tablet.
The weather show starts at quarter past four.
audio.caption: Write words or numbers

audio.transcript:
Examiner: Listen and Choose the correct answer.

At robot club the children are learning how to build robots.
Maria uses a weather app every morning before school.
Our robot can play music when we clap our hands.
The forecast says it is going to rain tomorrow.
They use a tablet to film their school technology project.
This afternoon they are going to test the new drone in the playground.
audio.caption: Choose the correct answer

audio.transcript:
Examiner: Listen and colour or draw.

Colour the computer screen blue, please.
Now colour the raincoat on the chair yellow.
Draw a cloud above the building.
Colour the tablet case red.
Draw a sun next to the window.
Draw a line from the girl to the microphone on her desk.
audio.caption: Colour and draw

questions.questionText: Match the word 'thunderstorm' to the description.

pairs.rightText: Storm with loud thunder and rain

questions.questionText: Match the word 'astronaut' to the description.
Match the word 'keyboard' to the description.

pairs.rightText: Part of a computer with letters on it

questions.questionText: Match the word 'firefighter' to the description.
Match the word 'smartphone' to the description.

pairs.rightText: Small phone you can carry in your pocket

questions.questionText: Match the word 'lightning' to the description.

pairs.rightText: Bright flash of light in the sky during a storm

questions.questionText: Read: "The thunderstorm is very loud tonight an…" Choose the best answer.

content.passage: The thunderstorm is very loud tonight and we can't sleep.

questions.questionText: Read: "My uncle is teaching computer skills at…" Choose the best answer.

content.passage: My uncle is teaching computer skills at our school.

questions.questionText: Read: "We watch the weather news on TV every ev…" Choose the best answer.

content.passage: We watch the weather news on TV every evening after dinner.

questions.questionText: Read: "My mum wears a blue uniform when she wor…" Choose the best answer.

content.passage: My mum wears a blue uniform when she works at the hospital.

questions.questionText: Read: "The robot is moving around the classroom…" Choose the best answer.

content.passage: The robot is moving around the classroom and saying hello.

questions.questionText: Read: "It's snowing heavily so we can't ride ou…" Choose the best answer.

content.passage: It's snowing heavily so we can't ride our bikes to school today.

questions.questionText: Read the text beginning "Hi! I'm Sofia and I'm in the weathe…" and choose the best answer.

content.passage: Hi! I'm Sofia and I'm in the weather club at school. Every Friday we check the thermometer and write a short forecast for our class. Last week we said it would be windy and we were right! I want to be a weather reporter when I'm older.

choices.text: Sofia is in a weather club at school.
Sofia checks the weather on Mondays only.
Sofia wants to be a chef.

questions.questionText: Read the text beginning "Last Saturday our school had a tech…" and choose the best answer.

content.passage: Last Saturday our school had a technology fair. Students showed robots, tablets and a small drone. My team built a robot that could carry books. The judges gave us a prize for the best design. We were very proud.

choices.text: The school had a technology fair on Saturday.
The robot could swim in water.

questions.questionText: Read the text beginning "My dad started a new job at an offi…" and choose the best answer.

content.passage: My dad started a new job at an office last month. He designs websites on his computer every day. Sometimes he works from home when the weather is bad. He says his job is interesting but he must finish projects on time.

choices.text: Dad designs websites at his new job.
Dad drives a bus every day.

questions.questionText: Read the text beginning "Our teacher showed us a new app for…" and choose the best answer.

content.passage: Our teacher showed us a new app for learning English. You can listen to stories and record your voice. I use it for ten minutes after homework. The app tells me when I make a mistake with a word.

choices.text: The app helps students learn English.
The app is only for cooking recipes.

questions.questionText: Read the text beginning "Yesterday there was a big storm dur…" and choose the best answer.

content.passage: Yesterday there was a big storm during school. The lights went out for five minutes and we stayed in our classroom. Our teacher read us a story by torch light. When the storm finished, we went home early because the bus could not come.

choices.text: There was a storm at school yesterday.
The children went swimming during the storm.
School finished at the normal time.

questions.questionText: Read the text beginning "Next week our school is going to ha…" and choose the best answer.

content.passage: Next week our school is going to have a careers day. A pilot, a scientist and a web designer will visit our class. We are going to ask them questions about their jobs. I am going to ask the scientist about space because I love stars and planets.

choices.text: Different workers will visit the school next week.

questions.questionText: Read the scene below. Write one sentence about the thunderstorm.

content.prompt: Read the scene below. Write one sentence about the thunderstorm.
content.taskDescription: Scene: Dark clouds are over a town and lightning is flashing during a thunderstorm.

questions.questionText: Read the scene below. Write one sentence about the chef.

content.prompt: Read the scene below. Write one sentence about the chef in the kitchen.
content.taskDescription: Scene: A chef in a white hat is cooking pasta in a busy restaurant kitchen.

questions.questionText: Write a note to your friend about today's technology lesson.

content.prompt: Write a note to your friend about today's technology lesson.
content.taskDescription: Tell your friend about the lesson.

questions.questionText: Write a message to your friend about a rainy day at home.

content.prompt: Write a message to your friend about a rainy day at home.
content.taskDescription: Tell your friend what you did indoors.
requiredPoints: Say what the weather was like
Say what you will do tomorrow

questions.questionText: Read the scene below. Write two or three sentences about the computer lab.

content.prompt: Read the scene below. Write two or three sentences about the computer lab.
requiredPoints: Say what the children are doing
Say what technology you can see

questions.questionText: Read the scene below. Write two or three sentences about the rainy street.

content.prompt: Read the scene below. Write two or three sentences about the rainy street.
requiredPoints: Say what the weather is like

questions.questionText: Read the two weather differences scenes below. Tell your partner about the differences.

content.prompt: Read Scene A and Scene B below. Tell your partner about five differences between them.
content.taskDescription:
Scene A: A town street on a sunny day with people in T-shirts and open shop doors.
Scene B: The same street in heavy rain with umbrellas, wet roads, and closed café tables.
followUpQuestions: Is it sunny in Scene A?
How many clouds are in Scene B?
Are people wearing raincoats in Scene B?
Where is the rainbow in Scene A?
What is the boy holding in Scene B?

questions.questionText: Answer the examiner's questions about about you weather tech.

content.prompt: Answer the examiner's questions about yourself, the weather where you live, and technology you use.
followUpQuestions: What's your name and how old are you?
What's the weather like today where you live?
What technology do you use at home or school?
What job would you like to have when you are older?
Do you prefer sunny days or rainy days? Why?

questions.questionText: Use the story outline to tell the story of how Tom found his lost tablet.

content.prompt: Use the story outline to tell the story of how Tom found his lost tablet.
content.taskDescription:
Story outline:
1. Tom realises his tablet is missing and looks worried.
2. He searches his bag and asks his sister for help.
3. They find the tablet in the school library.
4. Tom smiles and thanks his sister.
followUpQuestions: Where did he find the tablet?
How did Tom feel at the end?
What did he do when he found it?

coverageAchieved.notes: Authored for M3.1 Gold Mock specification.
